name: Chipotle Rub
recipeIngredient: 5 dried chipotle chiles, stemmed, seeded, and deveined (wear rubber gloves)
5 ancho chiles, seeded and deveined (wear rubber gloves)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small heavy skillet, dry-roast oregano over moderate heat, shaking the skillet occasionally, until fragrant and beginning to brown, about 2 minutes. Transfer oregano to a small bowl and let cool completely.
2. In an electric coffee/spice grinder, grind the cooled oregano fine.
3. In a heavy skillet, heat oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Fry the chiles, 1 or 2 at a time, turning them until puffed and just beginning to brown, about 10 seconds. Do not let the chiles burn; they will become bitter. Transfer fried chiles to paper towels to drain and cool until crisp.
4. Wearing rubber gloves, break the cooled chiles into pieces and grind in batches with the coffee/spice grinder.
5. In a food processor, grind oregano and chiles with garlic and salt until the mixture is a shaggy, saltlike consistency. If the mixture seems moist, spread it on a large baking sheet in an even layer and dry in the middle of an oven set at its lowest temperature until no longer moist, about 1 hour. Wearing rubber gloves, break up any lumps with your fingers.
6. Store the chipotle rub in an airtight container; it keeps for 6 months. Regrind the rub before using. Makes about 3 1/4 cups. Serves 2 to 4.
